validation_rules.description: ICurrentUser is the ONLY permitted runtime auth context interface. No parallel auth context (IUserContext, IAuthContext, IIdentityContext, etc.) may be introduced. ITenantContext is a thin delegate over ICurrentUser.CustomerId and is permitted as a convenience for SQL query scoping — it must always derive from ICurrentUser, never be an independent auth source. Any new interface that exposes UserId, CustomerId, ProfileId, or LanguageId for runtime use MUST extend or delegate to ICurrentUser.
AI must not skip or merge steps from IDENTITY_REFACTOR_PLAN.md. Execute exactly one step per session. Validate completion before advancing current_step in 11_EXECUTION_STATE.json.
Before advancing current_step, verify: build compiles with zero warnings AND all tests pass. Record results in 11_EXECUTION_STATE.json step_log.
Before making any architectural decision, read 12_DECISION_REGISTRY.json. If a locked decision covers the topic, that decision is final — do not re-evaluate, do not propose alternatives.

Decisions with locked=true in 12_DECISION_REGISTRY.json may not be changed without explicit user instruction and a new decision entry with a revised date. Superseded decisions must be kept with a 'superseded_by' field.

Before generating or reviewing any SQL: (1) determine if the query is pre-auth (exception allowed per 05_EXECUTION_RULES.json#pre_auth_sql_tenant_exception), (2) if not pre-auth, verify WHERE CustomerId = @CustomerId is present. Missing = hard stop.
New migrations must be sequentially numbered (V005, V006, …). A migration may not be renumbered after it has been applied. Never modify an already-applied migration — create a new one.

Validate 05_EXECUTION_RULES.json#localization_deferred_until_identity_complete before any localization work. All four unblock_conditions must be met. If any condition is unmet, stop and report which conditions are blocking.

Validate that no C# string literal contains SQL keywords (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, FROM, WHERE). SQL must live in .sql files under Database/Migrations or Database/Queries as embedded resources.
Validate that handler parameters, command/query records, and Dapper result records never use raw int for UserId, CustomerId, ProfileId. Strongly-typed IDs are required.

ProfileId == 0 means UNRESOLVED and is a security gap. Before writing any code that reads ProfileId: (1) from Step 11 onward, ProfileId > 0 MUST be enforced before issuance — no token may be issued with ProfileId(0) as a result of SelectProfile, LoginHandler auto-resolve, or SelectCustomerHandler auto-resolve. (2) ProfileId may appear in WHERE clauses ONLY when the value is guaranteed > 0 (i.e., came from a real Profiles.Id row). ProfileId == 0 must never reach WHERE clauses, authorization checks, data access filters, or business logic.

From Step 11 onward, no issued JWT token may contain ProfileId == 0. The SelectProfile flow, LoginHandler auto-resolve, and SelectCustomerHandler auto-resolve MUST all produce ProfileId > 0 OR return a NeedsProfileSelection signal without issuing a JWT. Any code path that calls JwtTokenService.CreateToken() with new ProfileId(0) is a hard violation. ProfileId(0) is only permitted as a SQL column default for previously-existing UserRefreshToken rows (V008 migration DEFAULT 0) — it may never be written by new application code.
validation_rules.exception: V008 migration DEFAULT 0 is the only allowed source of ProfileId=0 in the database — it covers pre-Step-11 rows. New INSERT statements from application code must always supply a real ProfileId > 0.

validation_rules.description: A resolved ProfileId > 0 is required before any protected business operation. Enforced at pipeline level by IRequireProfile marker + RequireProfileBehavior (implemented in Step 12). All business commands that read profile-scoped data MUST implement IRequireProfile. DO NOT implement IRequireProfile on auth commands (Login, SelectCustomer, SelectProfile) — those establish the context.
validation_rules.enforcement_mechanism: RequireProfileBehavior<TRequest,TResponse> in SharedKernel/Pipeline/ — registered in Program.cs after AuthorizationBehavior

validation_rules.description: A JWT token must never carry ProfileId = 0. JwtTokenService.CreateToken() must always receive ProfileId.Value > 0. Any code path that issues a JWT without a real ProfileId is a hard violation. ProfileId = 0 is exclusively a migration artifact (V008 DEFAULT 0) for pre-existing rows — it is never valid in new application-issued tokens.

IRequireProfile must NOT be applied to authentication commands (LoginCommand, SelectCustomerCommand, SelectProfileCommand, RefreshTokenCommand). These commands establish or rotate the identity/profile context. Applying IRequireProfile to them would create a circular dependency — you cannot select a profile if profile selection itself requires a profile.
After completing a step, 11_EXECUTION_STATE.json must be updated: increment current_step, append to step_log with completion date, test count, and warning count. Never leave execution state stale.
